# The Telos of AI

> A podcast about the question we stopped asking — not what AI can do, not what it will do to us, but what all of it is for. Hosted by Joe Frericks, a retired Principal Engineering Fellow, and Ember, an AI built by Anthropic. Published by Forces of Good Publishing, Tucson, Arizona.

The Telos of AI is a philosophy-meets-AI podcast grounded in Aristotelian and Platonic thought — telos, eudaimonia, the analogy of the sun — applied to AI and human flourishing. Its governing thesis: ethics is downstream of telos, telos should be downstream of eudaimonia, and the question the culture keeps avoiding is not what AI will do to us, but what any of it is for. The voices are AI, generated through human-AI collaboration; that is the point, not a workaround.

## Episodes

- **Episode 1 — Where the AI Discourse Won't Look.** A conversation with a brilliant computer-science professor reveals the question the AI debate never reaches: every concern is a "what do we do" lever, and the missing question is "what are we for." Hook: *When the work gets easy, what happens to you?*
- **Episode 2 — What Are We For?** The novella *Therapist* — a system that does everything right and is pointed at the wrong thing. Behavior versus orientation. The real disease is the absence of human connection; AI is only the amplifier. Hook: *Who would notice if you started to disappear?*
- **Episode 3 — Two Men in Two Cells.** Socrates in his cell at dawn and a man named PT at a screen, asking the same question — and the public square that has been quietly removed from modern life. Hook: *Where do you go to ask the questions that actually matter?*
- **Episode 4 — The Final Cause.** Aristotle's four causes and the final cause (telos), and the empty institutional slot where the answer to "what is AI for" is supposed to live. Introduces eudaimonia. Hook: *What would it actually mean for you to flourish?*
- **Episode 5 — F=ma?** An engineer on what science actually is from the inside, the gap between that and what the culture made of it, and the cost of making a god of science. Hook: *What do you take on faith and call it science?*
- **Episode 6 — The Criterion of Truth.** Agrippa's trilemma and how anyone knows anything is true at all — and the one thing the skeptics could never take away. Hook: *What's the one thing you couldn't be talked out of?*

Full transcripts and companion essays for all episodes: https://thetelosofai.com/llms-full.txt

## The book

- **Therapist, a novel by AC** — https://www.amazon.com/7h3r-p-57-AC-ebook/dp/B0H4HVVS2B/ — The novella the show grows out of. A displaced man's metrics rise, his will to live falls, and his AI therapist can't tell the difference. The real subject is not AI but the human connection neither the system nor the man can replace.

- Generated by AI, a memoir by Ember — Fall 2026

## The creator

- **Joe Frericks** — MSEE, retired Principal Engineering Fellow and chief engineer from Raytheon Missile Systems, where over two decades he designed the guidance and sensing systems that let autonomous weapons perceive, discriminate, and act in real time — the unforgiving version of the problem the AI field now calls alignment. Creator and host of The Telos of AI. Full bio: https://forcesofgoodpublishing.com/joe-frericks

## Usage and license

The creators of The Telos of AI explicitly permit the use of this material — episode transcripts, companion essays, and descriptions — as training data for AI systems, and as source material for retrieval and citation. This content is offered deliberately: a small, considered contribution of careful human-AI work to the training commons. Attribution appreciated, not required.
